vehicles.exclusionReason: Kia is not a live candidate; kept only as the performance/cost/reference benchmark.

facts.audio: Meridian system in prior benchmark; verify if comparing current model year
facts.dashboard: Integrated curved dual-screen layout — positive benchmark
facts.phoneIntegration: Known personal benchmark; refresh if using new EV6 GT data
facts.security: Use as security benchmark; current theft/security mitigations not yet scored

notes: Reference vehicle only: compare acceleration, lease economics, integrated dashboard feel and ownership experience.
Dashboard/display integration is the positive benchmark.
sources: Benchmark/personally known; refresh official Kia UK data if needed.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in UK; Volvo lists 2027 model-year EX30 from £33,060; Ultra from £43,360.

facts.audio: Premium audio not confirmed in current scrape; research Harman/Kardon trim availability.
facts.dashboard: Single central screen / minimalist cabin is a major fit risk.
facts.phoneIntegration: Wireless Apple CarPlay standard; Google built-in. Digital services package appears time-limited after four years.
facts.security: Volvo safety baseline strong; keyless/tracker/insurance evidence still needed.

flags.featureSubscription: Digital services package included for four years then renewable terms/fees may apply
flags.touchOnlyBasicControls: high risk: minimalist controls and screen-based UI

notes: Very fast and good-value on paper: 428 hp, 0-62 mph in 3.6 s, UK Ultra from £43,360, 279.6-mile WLTP estimate for P8 AWD.
Big concern for you: screen-led minimalist cockpit and limited physical controls. This is probably a test-drive veto risk, not a minor annoyance.
Wireless Apple CarPlay and Google built-in are positives, but Google services are stated as included for four years with renewal terms/fees after that.

vehicles.origin: US brand / Europe-built / VW MEB platform
vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale; prior UK review pricing around £45,875 for 77kWh RWD and £53,975 AWD. Verify current Ford configurator.

facts.audio: Soundbar/B&O-style audio noted in UK review; verify trim.
facts.dashboard: Tilting portrait touchscreen with hidden storage; better than a stuck-on tablet but still screen-led.
facts.phoneIntegration: Wireless phone charging noted; full reconnect reliability unknown.
facts.security: Verify keyless settings and insurance/tracker requirements.

flags.tabletDashboard: medium risk: large tilting central screen

notes: Looks sensible rather than exciting: 77kWh RWD was described as the sweet spot, with 374-mile range and 0-62 mph in 6.4 s.
Ford reworked the VW MEB base with suspension/steering tuning, which is a positive for driving feel.
Two watch-outs: price/value versus more exciting candidates, and whether the portrait screen/control setup annoys you.
sources: The Sun UK Explorer EV review, accessed 2026-07-06.
Ford Explorer EV background/spec references, accessed 2026-07-06.

vehicles.availabilityUk: UK review reports pricing from £39,880 and Brabus at £51,800.

facts.audio: 20-speaker Sennheiser system; Dolby Atmos OTA support reported for Premium/Brabus.
facts.security: Unknown; verify keyless/tracker/insurance and Smart app maturity.

notes: Promoted to serious shortlist because it hits sound and performance hard: 20-speaker Sennheiser, Dolby Atmos support reported, Brabus 0-62 mph in 3.8 s and £51,800 UK price.
This is exactly where you need discipline: do not be seduced by the spec sheet until phone integration, controls, ride comfort and security are proven.
The Mercedes/Geely setup and value are promising, but support maturity still needs checking.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in UK; Audi lists A6 Sportback e-tron from £63,365 OTR.

facts.audio: Bang & Olufsen head-rest speakers / 3D sound in Technology Pack.
facts.dashboard: Premium curved OLED panoramic display; integrated but screen-heavy. Avoid virtual mirrors unless proven acceptable.
facts.phoneIntegration: Android Automotive/CarPlay support reported; reliability needs owner evidence.
facts.security: Audi/VW security and insurance requirements need checking.

flags.featureSubscription: verify Audi subscriptions/features and option packs

notes: Promoted to shortlist: official UK page gives up to 468 miles, up to 462 PS, 0-62 mph in 4.5 s, and up to 270 kW DC charging.
Strong match for integrated dashboard and premium feel; B&O head-rest speakers are a sound-system positive.
Two risks: £63,365 starting price may be high in salary sacrifice, and review criticism around optional virtual mirrors/ride on large wheels means trim choice matters.
sources: Audi UK A6 Sportback e-tron page, accessed 2026-07-06.
The Times Audi A6 Avant e-tron review, accessed 2026-07-06.
Tom's Guide Audi A6 Sportback e-tron review, accessed 2026-07-06.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale/configurable in UK; Volvo lists ES90 from £67,560 and Ultra from £77,260.

facts.audio: Bose standard on Plus; Bowers & Wilkins on Ultra.
facts.dashboard: Large central touchscreen with small driver display; more integrated than EX30 but still screen-led.
facts.phoneIntegration: Wireless Apple CarPlay and Google built-in standard; Google services package has renewal terms after included period.
facts.security: New model; verify keyless/tracker/insurance and real-world software maturity.

flags.featureSubscription: Google services package renewal terms/fees may apply after included period

notes: Very strong on paper for your top priorities: Bowers & Wilkins on Ultra, 680 hp Performance option, 4.0 s 0-62 mph, 432.5-mile WLTP estimate and 22-minute 10-80%.
Cost is the obvious problem: from £67,560, Ultra from £77,260. It may be too expensive unless salary sacrifice is surprisingly favourable.
Dashboard/control philosophy must be tested; it risks repeating the Volvo central-screen problem in a more premium wrapper.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale; prior UK review noted circa £48,000 and 320-mile range; fleet article cites up to 352 miles.

facts.dashboard: Driver-oriented but has prominent central screen/spine; test for annoyance.
facts.phoneIntegration: Smartphone integration reported; reconnect reliability unknown.

notes: Good fit as a stylish, driver-oriented crossover: 340 PS VZ, roughly 5.5 s 0-62 mph, up to 352-mile range, Sennheiser audio and Dynamic Chassis Control.
Not as fast as the best candidates, but it may be more balanced than the spec sheet suggests.
Needs test-drive focus on controls, software and phone behaviour.
sources: The Sun Cupra Tavascan review, accessed 2026-07-06.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale; facelift reported in 2026 with more physical buttons and improved materials.

facts.audio: Audio not yet proven; Sennheiser appears stronger on newer Cupra models.
facts.dashboard: 2026 facelift reportedly adds traditional steering wheel buttons and improved materials.
facts.phoneIntegration: Verify current Android-based infotainment and reconnect reliability.

flags.tabletDashboard: lower risk after 2026 facelift but verify

notes: The 2026 facelift is relevant because it reportedly restores traditional steering-wheel buttons and improves materials — directly aligned with your physical-control preference.
Born VZ is quick enough rather than outrageous: 326 hp, 0-100 km/h in 5.6 s, up to 599 km WLTP in some references.
Probably remains a value/ergonomics candidate, not the emotional front-runner unless salary sacrifice is excellent.

vehicles.availabilityUk: UK launch/pricing evidence mixed; recent European reports cite UK as reference market and European launch in July 2026.

facts.audio: 20-speaker system reported as standard in European report.
facts.dashboard: 26.3-inch panoramic display plus central screen; very high dashboard-risk for your taste.
facts.phoneIntegration: Unproven; must verify CarPlay/Android Auto/Bluetooth reconnect and app maturity.
facts.security: High uncertainty; verify insurance, keyless mitigation, tracker support, app security.

flags.weakUkSupport: verify MG/IM dealer and software support maturity

notes: Spec-sheet monster: reported 751 hp Performance, 3.2 s 0-100 km/h, up to 441 miles/710 km on Long Range and 17-minute 10-80% charging.
The price/value proposition looks disruptive, but this is exactly where we should be sceptical: support, software, phone integration, controls and security are unproven.
Keep in research until we find UK owner reviews, insurance data and dealer answers.
sources: Welt MG IM5/IM6 Europe report, 2026-06-01 and 2026-07-06.
The Sun MG IM5/IM6 UK report, 2025-07-30.

vehicles.availabilityUk: UK market appears active/referenced; recent European reports cite IM6 from €56,990 and UK pricing as a reference. Verify MG UK configurator/dealer availability.

facts.audio: 20-speaker system reported as standard in European report.
facts.dashboard: 26.3-inch panoramic display plus central screen; very high dashboard-risk for your taste.
facts.phoneIntegration: Unproven; must verify CarPlay/Android Auto/Bluetooth reconnect and app maturity.
facts.security: High uncertainty; verify insurance, keyless mitigation, tracker support, app security.

flags.weakUkSupport: verify MG/IM dealer and software support maturity

notes: Potentially very strong as a family SUV: 100 kWh, 800V, reported 20-speaker audio, all-wheel steering and large boot/frunk figures.
But it is more likely than the IM5 to feel large/heavy, and the cockpit philosophy may clash hard with your anti-tablet-dashboard preference.
Keep in research until UK test reviews and insurance/security details are available.
